channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump picks Jay Bhattacharya, who backed COVID herd immunity, to lead National Institutes of Health
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen health economist Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, a critic of pandemic lockdowns and vaccine mandates, to lead the National Institutes of Health, the nation's leading medical research agency.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump team says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal brokered by Biden is actually Trump's win
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration kept President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration closely apprised of its efforts to broker the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hezbollah that took effect early Wednesday, according to the outgoing Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Kevin Hassett as National Economic Council head
item.description: President-elect Trump has chosen former White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett to lead the National Economic Council in his next administration, he said Tuesday. “I am very pleased to announce that Dr. Kevin A.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Jay Bhattacharya, prominent physician and economist, nominated by Trump for NIH director
item.description: Stanford-trained physician and economist Jay Bhattacharya has been nominated by President-elect Donald Trump to be the next director of the NIH.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's approval ratings jump in post-election poll, while Biden's figures sink to 4-year low
item.description: Poll figures for President Biden dropped to a four-year low in dissatisfaction from voters over how he is running the country, the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico suggests it would impose its own tariffs to retaliate against any Trump tariffs
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Claudia Sheinbaum suggested Tuesday that Mexico could retaliate with tariffs of its own, after U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatened to impose 25% import duties on Mexican goods if the country doesn’t stop the flow of drugs and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: After delay, Trump signs agreement with Biden White House to begin formal transition handoff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday reached a required agreement with President Joe Biden’s White House to allow his transition staff to coordinate with the existing federal workforce before taking office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump jokingly offers child "millions" for hair during Florida golf outing
item.description: President-elect Trump offered a child millions of dollars to purchase a child's hair as a joke on Monday at his Florida golf course.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris' campaign leaders say there was a 'price to be paid' for shortened campaign against Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The leaders of Kamala Harris' presidential campaign insist they simply didn't have enough time to execute a winning strategy against Donald Trump, pointing to “ferocious" political headwinds that were ultimately too much to overcome in the 107-day period after President Joe Biden ste
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won about 2.5M more votes this year than he did in 2020. This is where he did it
item.description: It’s a daunting reality for Democrats: Republican Donald Trump's support has grown broadly since he last sought the presidency. In his defeat of Democrat Kamala Harris, Trump won a bigger percentage of the vote in each one of the 50 states, and Washington, D.C., than he did four years ago.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, MAYA SWEEDLER, PARKER KAUFMANN and HUMERA LODHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas offers Trump administration more land and support for mass deportations
item.description: Two events in the Rio Grande Valley on Tuesday made clear Texas officials will work with the Trump administration on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Glenn Youngkin 'personally invites' new Trump admin to settle in Virginia over Maryland and DC
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin dropped a video inviting the Trump transition team and new administration members to settle in Virginia -- just across the Potomac from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump rounds out his second Cabinet
item.description: President-elect Trump is rounding out his Cabinet just a few weeks after securing a second White House term, filling the top jobs that will carry out his agenda in the years to come.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Post-election poll: Trump victory, cabinet choices, war, and aliens through voters' eyes
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's popularity is on the rise and the voters who went against him think World War III is likely.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Minnesota abortion access in federal court
item.description: Minnesota has some of the most accommodating laws granting access to abortion. A lawsuit filed late last week seeks to upend them.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, Anna Haecherl, and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Trump transition: Poll shows people approve of picks for new admin
item.description: A CBS News poll shows 59% of people surveyed approve of the way Trump is handling his transition to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Jessica Tisch to become 2nd woman to lead NYPD
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sanitation Commissioner Jessica Tisch became the top cop in New York City on Monday. She's the second woman to ever be the NYPD commissioner.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee to ask Gabbard 'lots of questions': Lankford
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said Sunday that the Senate Intelligence Committee will ask President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), “lots of questions.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: The rising price of paying the national debt is a risk for Trump’s promises on growth and inflation
item.description: Donald Trump has big plans for the economy. He also has big debt problem that’ll be a hurdle to delivering on those plan. Trump has bold ambitions on tax cuts, tariffs and other programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Anti-abortion groups sue Illinois officials over state insurance law
item.description: The federal lawsuit was filed Wednesday by the Thomas More Society on behalf of six anti-abortion groups.
item.dc:creator: Theodora Koulouvaris, NewsNation

item.title: Applicants sue Minnesota’s Office of Cannabis Management over disqualification
item.description: The OCM turned to a lottery because more than 1,800 applicants had sought 282 licenses. This week, regulators disqualified two-thirds of the applicants for a variety of reasons.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Feven Gerezgiher, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC
